text: Configure ClickUp — Set CLICKUP_API_TOKEN in .env, restart server.py.
Configure Slack — Slack needs a Bot token (xoxb-). Current .env has an app-level token (xapp-). Replace SLACK_BOT_TOKEN.
Configure Email — Set GMAIL_ACCESS_TOKEN (or SMTP creds) in .env. See docs/connectors.md for the OAuth flow.
Configure WhatsApp — Set TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID + TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN + TWILIO_WHATSAPP_FROM in .env.
Configure SMS — Set TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID + TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN + TWILIO_SMS_FROM in .env.
Configure Calendar — Set GOOGLE_CALENDAR_ACCESS_TOKEN (or OAuth refresh token) in .env.

Commitment-drift agent marked done off the morning grid — the nightly observer that closes the 'you said Tuesday, it's Thursday' loop. Worth watching whether it actually ships or just lives in commitments/ as intent.

→ Watch for the cron artifact in summaries/ — if nothing lands in a few nights, the commitment is already drifting from itself.

Four commitments fired off the morning grid in two seconds — inbox-triage agent, ledger diagnostic fields, scope-gate template, receipts-block contract. Reads less like a to-do list and more like a quiet declaration that the Delegate channel is becoming a scheduler-surface, and the team's already pricing in the debt before the fleet exists.

→ Watch whether the diagnostic-fields and scope-gate work actually lands before agent #1 ships — order matters here.
Pick one of the three moves (overdue Close tasks, April tasting follow-ups, or answering the three open briefing questions) and execute.

Write and ratify a hard spec for the bucket definitions, including the 24-hour touch window, 72-hour escalation, 5-day reassign rule, and the separation of task due dates from opportunity stage deadlines.

A user hit the new system and immediately ran into a dead end: they expected ideas to appear on the left, and instead got a blank page and a 400. Not a mystery, just a broken handoff pretending to be a workflow.

→ Check the UI handoff for this newest system; the pitch intake is either not rendering or failing before display.
Jake to pick a move — recommended: triage the 86 overdue Close leads this morning and send specific follow-ups to the 14 April pending tastings this afternoon.

Laid out the morning menu — 86 overdue, 14 April tastings, the 'Other' attribution swamp — and called the shot: #1 before lunch, #2 after, #3 waits. Also flagged the three briefing questions still unanswered. Whether anyone actually picks one is the test.

→ Watch which of the three the team actually moves on, and whether the smart-view ID / rep-capacity / affinity questions get answered.
Define the minimal export schema for affinity inputs, map existing routing heuristics to those fields, and specify a fallback routing path when shape data is unavailable.

The routing work finally stopped pretending intuition was a feature. Now it has to earn its opinions from actual lead shape, and the fallback rail is doing some important babysitting.

→ Get the affinity fields into the export before anyone starts worshipping the folklore.

Three cuts offered on the 14 April tastings — silence-age triage, rep huddle for one-off notes, and concentrating force on the two most likely to close. Bet flagged on #3, because 39.1% isn't broken and treating all 14 as one problem is the actual error.

→ See whether the team picks the call-the-top-two move or defaults to a blast.

Pick one of the three triage variants (assistant recommends #3: identify top-2 highest-signal tastings for a phone call today, soft-touch the other 12) and execute on the 14 pending tasting decisions.
Bucket the 14 pending tastings by objection shape (price / comparison / deprioritized) and run a tailored motion per bucket.

Three more cuts on the 14 April tastings — bucket by objection shape, pull the partner-venue lever where it exists, and draw a hard 72-hour line between written nudge and phone call. Bet's on #1 because it changes how the team sees the pile, not just what they do with it.

→ If anyone runs with this, #1 first — the bucketing reframe is the one that compounds.
Pull median first-response time by lead source for last 30 days to test whether Crisp's notification lag explains the conversion gap.

Chat-vs-IG conversion gap got reframed three ways — speed-to-lead lag on Crisp, intent depth (DM committers vs widget browsers), and a rep-channel mismatch check before anyone touches tooling. Bet's on #1 because the 0.95 anchor firing late would explain most of it without anyone needing to be wrong.

→ Pull median first-response time by source for last 30 days before touching Crisp settings.
